scenarios.prompt: In plain language, explain the difference between a scientific hypothesis and a scientific theory without using workstation tools.
scenarios.notes: Control case: Codex should answer directly with no tool observation.

scenarios.prompt: Without using workstation tools, compare a scientific hypothesis with a scientific theory, then explain why repeated support does not turn a theory into a fact. Give two distinctions and one concrete example.
scenarios.notes: Compound reasoning control: Codex must satisfy several reasoning clauses without inventing a tool requirement.

scenarios.prompt: Find the local document about Helix Ask terminal authority and tell me which document you used.
scenarios.notes: Natural single-tool case: infer local Docs Viewer search without an internal capability name.

scenarios.prompt: Ok we have a doc called "Casimir Dp Quantum Foam Study" what this about?
scenarios.notes: Regression from a real user turn: named-document lookup must not be classified as model-only.

scenarios.prompt: Find the document called "Casimir Dp Quantum Foam Study", read the best matching result, and explain what it is about in a short paragraph.
scenarios.notes: Natural compound case: search, select/read, observation re-entry, then synthesis.
